documentation.readme: 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool
usage_sections: Installation: Please see the installation instructions on the wiki (in development)

arguments.name: --aesKey
arguments.description: AES key to use for Kerberos Authentication (128 or 256 bits)

arguments.name: --continue-on-success
arguments.description: continues authentication attempts even after successes

arguments.name: --dns-server
arguments.description: Specify DNS server (default: Use hosts file & System DNS)

arguments.name: --dns-tcp
arguments.description: Use TCP instead of UDP for DNS queries

arguments.name: --fail-limit
arguments.description: max number of failed login attempts per host

arguments.name: --gfail-limit
arguments.description: max number of global failed login attempts

arguments.name: -id
arguments.description: database credential ID(s) to use for authentication

arguments.name: --ignore-pw-decoding
arguments.description: Ignore non UTF-8 characters when decoding the password file

arguments.name: --jitter
arguments.description: sets a random delay between each authentication

arguments.name: --kdcHost
arguments.description: FQDN of the domain controller. If omitted it will use the domain part (FQDN) specified in the target parameter

arguments.name: --log
arguments.description: export result into a custom file

arguments.name: --no-bruteforce
arguments.description: No spray when using file for username and password (user1 => password1, user2 => password2)

arguments.name: --no-progress
arguments.description: do not displaying progress bar during scan

arguments.name: --pem-cert
arguments.description: Use certificate authentication from PEM file

arguments.name: --pem-key
arguments.description: Private key for the PEM format

arguments.name: --pfx-base64
arguments.description: Use certificate authentication from pfx file encoded in base64

arguments.name: --pfx-cert
arguments.description: Use certificate authentication from pfx file .pfx

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: set how many concurrent threads to use

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: set how many concurrent threads to use

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: [DEPRECATED] no longer used, replaced by per-protocol timeouts (--smb-timeout, --ldap-timeout, --dcom-timeout)

arguments.name: --ufail-limit
arguments.description: max number of failed login attempts per username

arguments.name: --use-kcache
arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication from ccache file (KRB5CCNAME)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for NetExec (Pennyw0rth/NetExec). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for NetExec. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zmap/zmap (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the microsoft category. Fits when the engagement needs: 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pipx install git+$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool.

name: NetExec
summary: 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need microsoft, use NetExec because 🚩 This is the open source repository of NetExec maintained by a community of passionate people NetExec - The Network Execution Tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pipx install git+$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for NetExec.
When chaining microsoft tools, pair NetExec with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `NetExec --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
